stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of the Meta-Llama-3-8B model on Hugging Face.
It is categorized under key AI/ML domains such as 'transformers' and 'text-generation'.
The mention of Llama and Hugging Face indicates relevance within the open-source LLM ecosystem.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It pertains to the core field of large language models (LLMs) and text generation.
The associated tags indicate relevance to transformers, safetensors, and the Llama family of models.

stories.title: Cited but Not Verified: Parsing and Evaluating Source Attribution in LLM Deep Research Agents
stories.author: Corey Feld
bullet_points: LLM-powered research agents struggle with reliable source attribution, as current citation methods are unverifiable.
Existing solutions (self-citation or RAG) fail to validate source accessibility, relevance, or factual consistency.
The paper introduces a novel evaluation framework using an AST parser to address the critical issue of source attribution in LLM agents.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights a trending model, deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1, on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The model is categorized for text generation and conversational AI tasks.
It utilizes standard ML components like transformers and safetensors.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, has integrated MLX for performance optimization.
This integration specifically targets Apple Silicon, enhancing local AI inference speed on Mac devices.
The feature is currently available in a preview state, indicating ongoing development and optimization.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging applications.
It utilizes local AI coding agents, ensuring all processing runs directly on the user's device.
The core value proposition is providing private, on-device AI capabilities for coding assistance.

stories.title: OpenAI gpt-oss-safeguard
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama is collaborating with OpenAI and ROOST to deploy gpt-oss-safeguard reasoning models.
These models are designed for safety classification tasks and are available in 20B and 120B sizes.
The models are open-sourced under the permissive Apache 2.0 license, promoting accessibility.

stories.title: All the latest updates on AI data centers
stories.author: Justine Calma
bullet_points: Massive data centers are the physical infrastructure enabling the expansion of AI technologies.
The rapid growth of these energy-intensive facilities is causing global conflicts.
Key concerns surrounding AI data centers include strain on power grids, environmental impact, and community opposition.

stories.title: Apple reportedly has a deal to use Intel-made chips again
stories.author: Jay Peters
bullet_points: Apple is reportedly re-engaging with Intel for chip manufacturing, despite its successful shift to Apple Silicon.
The agreement is described as a 'preliminary agreement' reported by The Wall Street Journal.
This signals a potential strategic shift or diversification in Apple's hardware supply chain.

stories.title: Course correction: Google to link more sources in AI Overviews
stories.author: Ryan Whitwam
bullet_points: Google is enhancing its AI Overviews feature to improve source attribution.
The update involves linking to more sources within the AI-generated summaries.
This change addresses concerns regarding the transparency and accuracy of AI search results.

stories.title: ActCam: Zero-Shot Joint Camera and 3D Motion Control for Video Generation
stories.author: Baptiste Bellot-Gurlet
bullet_points: ActCam is a zero-shot method for video generation that allows fine-grained control over both character motion and camera trajectory.
It jointly transfers character motion from a source video into a new scene while enabling per-frame control of camera parameters (intrinsic and extrinsic).
The model is designed to build upon existing pretrained image-to-video diffusion models conditioned on scene depth.

stories.title: AI Co-Mathematician: Accelerating Mathematicians with Agentic AI
stories.author: Pushmeet Kohli
bullet_points: Introduction of an 'AI co-mathematician' workbench designed to assist mathematicians in open-ended research.
The system provides holistic support across the entire mathematical workflow, from ideation and literature search to theorem proving.
Key features include an asynchronous, stateful workspace that manages uncertainty and tracks failed hypotheses.

stories.title: ActCam: Zero-Shot Joint Camera and 3D Motion Control for Video Generation
stories.author: Baptiste Bellot-Gurlet
bullet_points: ActCam is a zero-shot method designed for fine-grained control in video generation, addressing both character motion and camera cinematography.
It jointly transfers character motion from a source video into a new scene while allowing per-frame control over camera intrinsic and extrinsic parameters.
The model leverages any pretrained image-to-video diffusion model, conditioning it using scene depth and other inputs.

stories.title: Why Global LLM Leaderboards Are Misleading: Small Portfolios for Heterogeneous Supervised ML
stories.author: Swati Gupta
bullet_points: The paper critiques current LLM leaderboards, which rely on pairwise human feedback (like Arena).
Analysis of 89K comparisons across 52 LLMs shows that the standard global Bradley-Terry (BT) ranking is misleading.
The study found that the top 50 models ranked by BT are statistically indistinguishable, with win probabilities barely exceeding 0.53.

stories.title: Superintelligent Retrieval Agent: The Next Frontier of Information Retrieval
stories.author: Anshumali Shrivastava
bullet_points: The paper addresses the limitation of current Retrieval-Augmented Agents (RAGs) which treat retrieval as a 'black box'.
Existing RAG methods rely on iterative, exploratory querying, mimicking a novice user rather than an expert.
The proposed 'Superintelligent Retrieval Agent' aims to improve efficiency by incorporating strong priors for expert-level navigation, reducing latency and unnecessary queries.

stories.title: AWS North Virginia data center outage – recovery to take hours
stories.author: christhecaribou
bullet_points: A significant outage occurred at the AWS North Virginia data center.
Recovery from the outage was reported to take several hours.
The incident impacted major services, including FanDuel and Coinbase.

stories.title: We Measured It: LSP Saves AI Agents 5-34x Tokens vs Grep
stories.author: daynablackwell
bullet_points: LSP (Language Server Protocol) significantly reduces token usage for AI agents compared to traditional 'grep' searches.
The study shows LSP saves 5-34x tokens across five different codebases.
The research highlights that grep results often suffer from high inaccuracy, with 92-99% being false positives.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The content references the 'bigscience/bloom' model, a significant large language model.
It is presented within the Hugging Face Benchmarks, indicating a focus on model performance and comparison.
The associated pipeline is 'text-generation', highlighting its primary application in NLP tasks.

stories.title: Subagents and web search in Claude Code
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama has integrated support for subagents within Claude Code.
The update also enables web search functionality within the Claude Code environment.
This suggests an enhancement in the capabilities of local LLM orchestration tools for complex tasks.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command for running coding tools locally or in the cloud.
It supports various coding models, including Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: Image generation (experimental)
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama is expanding its capabilities to include local image generation.
The feature is currently experimental and available on macOS.
Support for Windows and Linux platforms is planned for future releases.

stories.title: Claude Code with Anthropic API compatibility
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports the Anthropic Messages API.
This integration allows users to leverage tools like Claude Code within the Ollama ecosystem.
It bridges the gap between open-source local models and proprietary APIs like Anthropic's.

stories.title: OpenAI Codex with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama enables the use of OpenAI's Codex CLI with open-source models.
Codex functionality allows models to read, modify, and execute code within a working directory.
The integration supports various open-weight alternatives, such as gpt-oss:20b and gpt-oss:120b.

stories.title: Amazon is adding a vertical video feed to Prime Video
stories.author: Emma Roth
bullet_points: Amazon Prime Video is integrating a vertical, short-form video feed called 'Clips'.
This feature mirrors trends set by competitors like Netflix and Disney Plus.
Users can transition from the short clips to the full title, or rent/buy it.

stories.title: Here is Yarbo’s promise to fix the robot mower that ran me over
stories.author: Sean Hollister
bullet_points: Robot lawnmowers from Yarbo have been shown to be vulnerable to hacking.
The vulnerability allows hackers to access sensitive personal data, including GPS coordinates and Wi-Fi passwords.
Yarbo has issued a response/promise regarding the security flaw in their devices.

stories.title: Manufacturing qubits that can move
stories.author: John Timmer
bullet_points: The article discusses the manufacturing challenges associated with qubits that possess mobility.
A key technical hurdle identified is the difficulty in integrating electronic manufacturing processes with flexible geometries.
This suggests ongoing research into advanced fabrication techniques for next-generation quantum computing hardware.

stories.title: Sony says "efficient" AI tools will lead to even more games flooding the market
stories.author: Kyle Orland
bullet_points: Sony anticipates an influx of games due to the efficiency of AI tools.
The company emphasizes that human artists must remain central to the creative process.
The statement addresses the impact of AI on game development and market saturation.

stories.title: Chaos erupts as cyberattack disrupts learning platform Canvas amid finals
stories.author: Dan Goodin
bullet_points: A cyberattack disrupted the widely used learning platform, Canvas.
The incident occurred during a critical period, coinciding with year-end finals.
Schools and colleges nationwide were forced to postpone scheduled tests as a result.

stories.title: Elon Musk faces criminal probe in France after ignoring summons in X case
stories.author: Jon Brodkin
bullet_points: Elon Musk is facing a criminal probe in France.
The probe relates to an issue concerning his platform, X.
France is threatening criminal charges if Musk fails to appear for questioning.

stories.title: Chrome's 4GB AI model isn't new, but you're not wrong for being confused
stories.author: Ryan Whitwam
bullet_points: Chrome is utilizing a local 4GB AI model for on-device processing.
The article addresses user confusion regarding the significant storage footprint of this feature.
Users have the option to disable Chrome's local AI functionality.

stories.title: UniPool: A Globally Shared Expert Pool for Mixture-of-Experts
stories.author: Hong Cheng
bullet_points: Introduces UniPool, a novel approach to Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) architectures.
Challenges the conventional MoE design where each transformer layer has a separate, rigid expert set.
Suggests that replacing learned top-k routing with uniform random routing in deeper layers causes only minor accuracy degradation (1.0-1.6 points).

stories.title: Verifier-Backed Hard Problem Generation for Mathematical Reasoning
stories.author: Ning Miao
bullet_points: LLMs struggle to generate valid, challenging, and novel mathematical problems necessary for advanced training.
The proposed method, VHG, addresses the limitations of current approaches (human reliance or naive self-play) by incorporating verification.
This work aims to improve LLM capabilities in autonomous scientific research by providing a robust problem generation framework.

stories.title: Optimizer-Model Consistency: Full Finetuning with the Same Optimizer as Pretraining Forgets Less
stories.author: Tong Zhang
bullet_points: The research investigates the role of optimizers in the pretraining and finetuning of Large Language Models (LLMs).
The study observes that using the identical optimizer during full finetuning as was used during pretraining leads to a better learning-forgetting tradeoff.
This method reportedly outperforms other optimizers and even LoRA during the Supervised Finetuning (SFT) stage.

stories.title: When No Benchmark Exists: Validating Comparative LLM Safety Scoring Without Ground-Truth Labels
stories.author: Michael A. Riegler
bullet_points: Addresses the critical problem of comparing LLM safety when no established, labeled benchmarks exist for a specific context.
Introduces the concept of 'benchmarkless comparative safety scoring' to allow for pre-labeling evaluations.
Defines strict conditions (fixed scenario pack, rubric, auditor, etc.) under which scenario-based audits can serve as deployment evidence.

stories.title: Superintelligent Retrieval Agent: The Next Frontier of Information Retrieval
stories.author: Anshumali Shrivastava
bullet_points: Current retrieval-augmented agents often treat information retrieval as a 'black box' process.
Existing methods rely on iterative, exploratory querying, mimicking a novice user's search behavior.
The proposed advancement aims to move beyond this by incorporating 'strong priors' to emulate expert navigation, reducing latency and unnecessary queries.

stories.title: Are We Making Progress in Multimodal Domain Generalization? A Comprehensive Benchmark Study
stories.author: Olga Fink
bullet_points: The paper addresses the lack of clarity regarding genuine progress in Multimodal Domain Generalization (MMDG).
Current research suffers from fragmentation due to inconsistent evaluation protocols across studies.
Existing benchmarks are criticized for focusing too heavily on action recognition while neglecting real-world applications.

stories.title: StraTA: Incentivizing Agentic Reinforcement Learning with Strategic Trajectory Abstraction
stories.author: Zhenfei Yin
bullet_points: Addresses the challenge of long-horizon decision-making in LLM-based agents, where current methods are too reactive.
Introduces StraTA (Strategic Trajectory Abstraction), a framework to incorporate explicit trajectory-level strategy into agentic RL.
Aims to improve exploration and credit assignment over extended decision-making sequences.

stories.title: UniPool: A Globally Shared Expert Pool for Mixture-of-Experts
stories.author: Hong Cheng
bullet_points: Introduces UniPool, a novel approach to Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) architectures.
Challenges the conventional design where each transformer layer has its own isolated expert set.
Suggests that uniform random routing can perform nearly as well as learned routing in deeper layers, implying potential efficiency gains.

stories.title: Verifier-Backed Hard Problem Generation for Mathematical Reasoning
stories.author: Ning Miao
bullet_points: LLMs struggle to generate valid, challenging, and novel mathematical problems, hindering advanced training and autonomous research.
The paper introduces VHG, a new method to generate hard problems backed by formal verification.
VHG aims to overcome limitations of existing methods, which rely on expensive human experts or suffer from reward hacking in self-play.

stories.title: When No Benchmark Exists: Validating Comparative LLM Safety Scoring Without Ground-Truth Labels
stories.author: Michael A. Riegler
bullet_points: Addresses the critical problem of comparing LLM safety when no established benchmark or ground-truth labels exist.
Proposes a formal framework for 'benchmarkless comparative safety scoring' for real-world deployments.
Defines strict conditions (fixed scenario pack, rubric, auditor, etc.) under which scenario-based audits can serve as deployment evidence.

stories.title: Are We Making Progress in Multimodal Domain Generalization? A Comprehensive Benchmark Study
stories.author: Olga Fink
bullet_points: The paper addresses the lack of clarity regarding genuine progress in Multimodal Domain Generalization (MMDG).
Current research suffers from fragmentation due to inconsistent evaluation protocols across different studies.
Existing benchmarks are criticized for focusing too heavily on action recognition while neglecting other real-world applications.

stories.title: EMO: Pretraining Mixture of Experts for Emergent Modularity
stories.author: Sewon Min
bullet_points: The paper addresses the inefficiency of monolithic Large Language Models (LLMs) where the full model is used even for narrow tasks.
It discusses Mixture-of-Experts (MoEs) as a potential solution to activate only relevant model components.
The research highlights a practical limitation of MoEs: restricting inference to a subset of experts causes significant performance degradation.

stories.title: Verifier-Backed Hard Problem Generation for Mathematical Reasoning
stories.author: Ning Miao
bullet_points: The research addresses a key limitation of LLMs: their inability to generate valid, challenging, and novel mathematical problems.
Existing problem generation methods are either too reliant on human experts or suffer from invalid outputs due to reward hacking in self-play.
The proposed method, VHG, introduces a verifier-backed approach to solve the hard problem generation challenge for advancing LLM training.

stories.title: When No Benchmark Exists: Validating Comparative LLM Safety Scoring Without Ground-Truth Labels
stories.author: Michael A. Riegler
bullet_points: Addresses the critical problem of comparing LLM safety when no established benchmark or ground-truth labels exist for a specific context.
Introduces the concept of 'benchmarkless comparative safety scoring' to formalize this evaluation setting.
Defines strict conditions (fixed scenario pack, rubric, auditor, etc.) under which scenario-based audits can serve as deployment evidence.

stories.title: Beyond Negative Rollouts: Positive-Only Policy Optimization with Implicit Negative Gradients
stories.author: Hao Fang
bullet_points: The paper discusses advancements in Reinforcement Learning with Verifiable Rewards (RLVR) for improving LLM reasoning.
It analyzes the shift in policy optimization methods, noting the transition from PPO to Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO).
The research proposes an alternative approach to policy optimization by focusing on positive-only rollouts, addressing limitations of traditional negative rollouts.

stories.title: StraTA: Incentivizing Agentic Reinforcement Learning with Strategic Trajectory Abstraction
stories.author: Zhenfei Yin
bullet_points: Addresses the challenge of long-horizon decision-making in LLM-based agents, which are often purely reactive.
Introduces StraTA (Strategic Trajectory Abstraction), a framework to incorporate explicit trajectory-level strategies into agentic RL.
Aims to improve exploration and credit assignment over extended decision-making processes.

stories.title: Recursive Agent Optimization
stories.author: Graham Neubig
bullet_points: Introduces Recursive Agent Optimization (RAO), a new reinforcement learning technique.
RAO trains recursive agents capable of spawning and delegating sub-tasks to themselves.
This recursive structure enables agents to handle longer contexts and complex problems using a divide-and-conquer approach.

stories.title: Can RL Teach Long-Horizon Reasoning to LLMs? Expressiveness Is Key
stories.author: Abulhair Saparov
bullet_points: Addresses a key limitation in applying RL to LLMs: the lack of controlled, scalable environments for studying reasoning scaling.
Introduces 'ScaleLogic,' a synthetic framework designed to independently control two critical axes of reasoning difficulty: proof planning depth (horizon) and logical expressiveness.
Focuses on advancing the understanding of how LLMs can achieve long-horizon reasoning using Reinforcement Learning.

stories.title: MASPO: Joint Prompt Optimization for LLM-based Multi-Agent Systems
stories.author: Min Zhang
bullet_points: Introduces MASPO, a new framework for optimizing prompts in LLM-based Multi-Agent Systems (MAS).
Addresses the challenge of aligning local agent objectives with overall system goals in complex collaborative tasks.
Focuses on the difficulty of jointly optimizing role-specific prompts across interacting agents.

stories.title: Dirty Frag: Universal Linux LPE
stories.author: openwall.com via lattera
bullet_points: The story concerns a 'Dirty Frag,' which is a type of Linux Local Privilege Escalation (LPE) vulnerability.
The vulnerability is described as 'Universal,' suggesting it could affect a wide range of Linux systems.
The source (Lobsters) indicates this is a technical security disclosure, relevant to system administrators and security researchers.

stories.title: AI Is Breaking Two Vulnerability Cultures — And Vibe Coders Are About to Get Caught in the Middle
stories.author: layzerzero105
bullet_points: AI tools (like Claude/Cursor) are disrupting established vulnerability disclosure and bug bounty cultures.
The responsibility for security fallout from using these AI tools now rests with the developers/shippers.
The article provides three essential guardrails for developers navigating this new AI-driven security landscape.

stories.title: ai agent fluency is the new staff+ engineering skill
stories.author: pvgomes
bullet_points: Directing AI agents is emerging as a critical competency for senior (Staff+) engineers.
The article suggests that effective AI utilization requires more than just simple prompt engineering.
AI governance and strategic oversight are highlighted as being more important than superficial prompt tricks.
